page.content: In recent years, the Asia-Pacific region has experienced notable shifts in student financing dynamics, underscored by the increasing student loan default rates and corresponding tuition revenue metrics.

lines.text: In recent years, the Asia-Pacific region has experienced notable shifts in student financing dynamics, underscored by the
increasing student loan default rates and corresponding tuition revenue metrics.

page.content: As reflected in the data, the default rate among students rose from 6.2% in 2021 to 7.1% in 2022, highlighting a growing concern around student debt sustainability. Concurrently, the tuition revenue per student also saw an increase, with figures escalating from ₩1,200,000 in 2020 to ₩1,250,000 in 2021.

concern around student debt sustainability. Concurrently, the tuition revenue per student also saw an increase, with figures

page.content: This trend raises significant questions regarding the affordability of education and the economic pressures faced by students, particularly those from low-income backgrounds, where the default rate reached 10.5%, slightly below the anticipated 11.0%. Furthermore, total students enrolled in higher education institutions increased alongside these financial metrics, with the total student population estimated at 12.0% growth. As institutions adapt to these changing financial landscapes, the implications for educational policy and funding models will be critical for ensuring equitable access to higher education. For instance, the overall amount lent in student loans reached a significant benchmark, contributing to the total amount lent which recorded as 1.2MKRW. Understanding these interconnected factors will be essential for stakeholders aiming to foster a sustainable educational environment in the region.
